name: A curated list of data engineering tools for software developers
description: A curated list of data engineering tools for software developers

links.title: Percona XtraBackup
links.description: A free, open source, complete online backup solution for all versions of Percona Server, MySQL® and MariaDB®.

links.title: mysql_utils
links.description: Pinterest MySQL Management Tools.

links.title: Rivestack
links.description: Managed PostgreSQL with pgvector for AI workloads. HNSW indexing, sub-4ms latency, and built-in SQL editor with automatic embedding generation.

links.title: Amazon RDS
links.description: Makes it easy to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the cloud.

links.title: Crate.IO
links.description: Scalable SQL database with the NOSQL goodies.

links.title: Redis
links.description: An open source, BSD licensed, advanced key-value cache and store.

links.title: Riak
links.description: A distributed database designed to deliver maximum data availability by distributing data across multiple servers.

links.title: HyperDex
links.description: A scalable, searchable key-value store. Deprecated.

links.title: SSDB
links.description: A high performance NoSQL database supporting many data structures, an alternative to Redis.

links.title: Kyoto Tycoon
links.description: A lightweight network server on top of the Kyoto Cabinet key-value database, built for high-performance and concurrency.

links.title: IonDB
links.description: A key-value store for microcontroller and IoT applications.

links.title: Cassandra
links.description: The right choice when you need scalability and high availability without compromising performance.

links.title: Cassandra Calculator
links.description: This simple form allows you to try out different values for your Apache Cassandra cluster and see what the impact is for your application.

links.title: CCM
links.description: A script to easily create and destroy an Apache Cassandra cluster on localhost.

links.title: ScyllaDB
links.description: NoSQL data store using the seastar framework, compatible with Apache Cassandra.

links.title: HBase
links.description: The Hadoop database, a distributed, scalable, big data store.

links.title: AWS Redshift
links.description: A fast, fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse that makes it simple and cost-effective to analyze all your data using your existing business intelligence tools.

links.title: FiloDB
links.description: Distributed. Columnar. Versioned. Streaming. SQL.

links.title: Vertica
links.description: Distributed, MPP columnar database with extensive analytics SQL.

links.title: ClickHouse
links.description: Distributed columnar DBMS for OLAP. SQL.

links.title: MongoDB
links.description: An open-source, document database designed for ease of development and scaling.

links.title: Percona Server for MongoDB
links.description: Percona Server for MongoDB® is a free, enhanced, fully compatible, open source, drop-in replacement for the MongoDB® Community Edition that includes enterprise-grade features and functionality.

links.title: MemDB
links.description: Distributed Transactional In-Memory Database (based on MongoDB).

links.title: Couchbase
links.description: The highest performing NoSQL distributed database.

links.title: RethinkDB
links.description: The open-source database for the realtime web.

links.title: RavenDB
links.description: Fully Transactional NoSQL Document Database.

links.title: Neo4j
links.description: The world's leading graph database.

links.title: Omnigraph
links.description: Typed graph database where agents branch and merge like Git. S3-native, Rust, traversal + vector + BM25 in one runtime.

links.title: OrientDB
links.description: 2nd Generation Distributed Graph Database with the flexibility of Documents in one product with an Open Source commercial friendly license.

links.title: Titan
links.description: A scalable graph database optimized for storing and querying graphs containing hundreds of billions of vertices and edges distributed across a multi-machine cluster.

links.title: FlockDB
links.description: A distributed, fault-tolerant graph database by Twitter. Deprecated.

links.title: Actionbase
links.description: A database for user interactions (likes, views, follows) represented as graphs, with precomputed reads served in real-time.

links.title: DAtomic
links.description: The fully transactional, cloud-ready, distributed database.

links.title: Gaffer
links.description: A large-scale graph database.

links.title: InfluxDB
links.description: Scalable datastore for metrics, events, and real-time analytics.

links.title: OpenTSDB
links.description: A scalable, distributed Time Series Database.

links.title: QuestDB
links.description: A relational column-oriented database designed for real-time analytics on time series and event data.

links.title: kairosdb
links.description: Fast scalable time series database.

links.title: Heroic
links.description: A scalable time series database based on Cassandra and Elasticsearch, by Spotify.

links.title: Druid
links.description: Column oriented distributed data store ideal for powering interactive applications.

links.title: Riak-TS
links.description: Riak TS is the only enterprise-grade NoSQL time series database optimized specifically for IoT and Time Series data.

links.title: Akumuli
links.description: A numeric time-series database. It can be used to capture, store and process time-series data in real-time. The word "akumuli" can be translated from esperanto as "accumulate".

links.title: Rhombus
links.description: A time-series object store for Cassandra that handles all the complexity of building wide row indexes.

links.title: Dalmatiner DB
links.description: Fast distributed metrics database.

links.title: Blueflood
links.description: A distributed system designed to ingest and process time series data.

links.title: Timely
links.description: A time series database application that provides secure access to time series data based on Accumulo and Grafana.

links.title: Tarantool
links.description: An in-memory database and application server.

links.title: GreenPlum
links.description: The Greenplum Database (GPDB) - An advanced, fully featured, open source data warehouse. It provides powerful and rapid analytics on petabyte scale data volumes.

links.title: cayley
links.description: An open-source graph database. Google.

links.title: Snappydata
links.description: OLTP + OLAP Database built on Apache Spark.

links.title: TimescaleDB
links.description: Built as an extension on top of PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB is a time-series SQL database providing fast analytics, scalability, with automated data management on a proven storage engine.

links.title: chDB
links.description: Embedded ClickHouse — full ClickHouse SQL dialect, ~80 data formats, and 12+ source connectors (S3, Postgres, MongoDB, Kafka, Iceberg) in core. Python, Go, Rust, Node, Bun, Zig, and Ruby bindings.

links.title: datacompy
links.description: A Python library that facilitates the comparison of two DataFrames in Pandas, Polars, Spark and more. The library goes beyond basic equality checks by providing detailed insights into discrepancies at both row and column levels.

links.title: dvt
links.description: Data Validation Tool compares data from source and target tables to ensure that they match. It provides column validation, row validation, schema validation, custom query validation, and ad hoc SQL exploration.

links.title: koala-diff
links.description: A high-performance Python library for comparing large datasets (CSV, Parquet) locally using Rust and Polars. It features zero-copy streaming to prevent OOM errors and generates interactive HTML data quality reports.

links.title: FutureSearch SDK
links.description: Python SDK that dispatches parallel web-research agents across

links.title: DataSpoc Pipe
links.description: Data ingestion engine that connects 400+ Singer taps to Parquet files in cloud buckets (S3, GCS, Azure). Streaming, incremental, with auto-catalog.

links.title: Enrich.sh
links.description: Managed event ingestion service that converts JSON sent to a REST API into Hive-partitioned Parquet on Cloudflare R2, queryable from DuckDB, ClickHouse, BigQuery, Snowflake, and Python.

links.title: enrich-companies
links.description: CLI tool to enrich CSV files with company data (financials, contacts, metadata) from 250M+ company records. Available on [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/enrich-companies).

links.title: ingestr
links.description: CLI tool to copy data between databases with a single command. Supports 50+ sources including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Salesforce, Shopify to any data warehouse.

links.title: BottledWater
links.description: Change data capture from PostgreSQL into Kafka. Deprecated.

links.title: kafkat
links.description: Simplified command-line administration for Kafka brokers.

links.title: kafkacat
links.description: Generic command line non-JVM Apache Kafka producer and consumer.

links.title: pg-kafka
links.description: A PostgreSQL extension to produce messages to Apache Kafka.

links.title: kafka-docker
links.description: Kafka in Docker.

links.title: kafka-manager
links.description: A tool for managing Apache Kafka.

links.title: kafka-node
links.description: Node.js client for Apache Kafka 0.8.

links.title: Secor
links.description: Pinterest's Kafka to S3 distributed consumer.

links.title: Kafka-logger
links.description: Kafka-winston logger for Node.js from Uber.

links.title: Kroxylicious
links.description: A Kafka Proxy, solving problems like encrypting your Kafka data at rest.

links.title: AWS Kinesis
links.description: A fully managed, cloud-based service for real-time data processing over large, distributed data streams.

links.title: RabbitMQ
links.description: Robust messaging for applications.

links.title: dlt
links.description: A fast&simple pipeline building library for Python data devs, runs in notebooks, cloud functions, airflow, etc.

links.title: drt
links.description: OSS Reverse ETL CLI. Sync data from warehouses to business tools via YAML.

links.title: FluentD
links.description: An open source data collector for unified logging layer.

links.title: Embulk
links.description: An open source bulk data loader that helps data transfer between various databases, storages, file formats, and cloud services.

links.title: Apache Sqoop
links.description: A tool designed for efficiently transferring bulk data between Apache Hadoop and structured datastores such as relational databases.

links.title: Heka
links.description: Data Acquisition and Processing Made Easy. Deprecated.

links.title: Gobblin
links.description: Universal data ingestion framework for Hadoop from LinkedIn.

links.title: Nakadi
links.description: An open source event messaging platform that provides a REST API on top of Kafka-like queues.

links.title: Pravega
links.description: Provides a new storage abstraction - a stream - for continuous and unbounded data.

links.title: AWS Data Wrangler
links.description: Utility belt to handle data on AWS.

links.title: Airbyte
links.description: Open-source data integration for modern data teams.

links.title: DBConvert Streams
links.description: self-hosted database migration and change data capture (CDC) tool with built-in SQL IDE.

links.title: Artie
links.description: Real-time data ingestion tool leveraging change data capture.

links.title: Sling
links.description: CLI data integration tool specialized in moving data between databases, as well as storage systems.

links.title: Meltano
links.description: CLI & code-first ELT.

links.title: Singer SDK
links.description: The fastest way to build custom data extractors and loaders compliant with the Singer Spec.

links.title: Google Sheets ETL
links.description: Live import all your Google Sheets to your data warehouse.

links.title: CsvPath Framework
links.description: A delimited data preboarding framework that fills the gap between MFT and the data lake.

links.title: Estuary Flow
links.description: No/low-code data pipeline platform that handles both batch and real-time data ingestion.

links.title: db2lake
links.description: Lightweight Node.js ETL framework for databases → data lakes/warehouses.

links.title: data-genie
links.description: High-performance, streaming-first ETL engine for Node.js and TypeScript with constant memory footprint.

links.title: Kreuzberg
links.description: Polyglot document intelligence library with a Rust core and bindings for Python, TypeScript, Go, and more. Extracts text, tables, and metadata from 62+ document formats for data pipeline ingestion.

links.title: pdfmux
links.description: Python PDF-to-Markdown orchestrator. Classifies each page and routes to the optimal backend (PyMuPDF, Docling, RapidOCR, Gemini Flash), emitting Markdown plus a per-page confidence score so ingestion pipelines can quarantine low-trust pages before feeding LLMs or retrieval.

links.title: DataRaven
links.description: Managed cloud object storage transfers for ingestion workflows.

links.title: Xquik
links.description: Real-time X (Twitter) data extraction platform with REST API (76 endpoints), 20 bulk extraction tools, account monitoring, HMAC-signed webhooks, and MCP server for AI agent integration.

links.title: Arpe.io
links.description: High-speed CLI tools for database export, import, replication and migration with parallel streaming to CSV, Parquet, JSON and cloud storage, supporting PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server and 80+ sources.

links.title: Crustdata
links.description: A real-time B2B data API for company and people intelligence, providing firmographics, headcount signals, job listings, web traffic, and funding events via REST API and webhooks for data enrichment pipelines.

links.title: crdt-merge
links.description: Conflict-free merge for DataFrames, JSON, ML models & distributed agents — powered by CRDTs.

links.title: LinkedIn Jobs Scraper
links.description: Crawlee-based actor extracting structured LinkedIn job listings at scale without API keys.

links.title: CARQ
links.description: Context-Aware RAG Processing Queue for high availability and adaptive rate-limiting.

links.title: Rawbbit
links.description: Open-source self-hosted analytics pipeline that lands raw events as Parquet in your own object storage. Uses NATS JetStream for durable buffering and BigQuery external tables for querying. Designed for teams that want to own their raw event data.

links.title: faucet-stream
links.description: Config-driven data-movement platform for Rust with pluggable source and sink connectors, running ETL, CDC, and streaming pipelines declaratively from YAML or embedded as a library.

links.title: HDFS
links.description: A distributed file system designed to run on commodity hardware.

links.title: Snakebite
links.description: A pure python HDFS client.

links.title: AWS S3
links.description: Object storage built to retrieve any amount of data from anywhere.

links.title: smart_open
links.description: Utils for streaming large files (S3, HDFS, gzip, bz2).

links.title: Alluxio
links.description: A memory-centric distributed storage system enabling reliable data sharing at memory-speed across cluster frameworks, such as Spark and MapReduce.

links.title: CEPH
links.description: A unified, distributed storage system designed for excellent performance, reliability, and scalability.

links.title: OrangeFS
links.description: Orange File System is a branch of the Parallel Virtual File System.

links.title: SnackFS
links.description: A bite-sized, lightweight HDFS compatible file system built over Cassandra.

links.title: GlusterFS
links.description: Gluster Filesystem.

links.title: XtreemFS
links.description: Fault-tolerant distributed file system for all storage needs.

links.title: S3QL
links.description: A file system that stores all its data online using storage services like Google Storage, Amazon S3, or OpenStack.

links.title: LizardFS
links.description: Software Defined Storage is a distributed, parallel, scalable, fault-tolerant, Geo-Redundant and highly available file system.

links.title: AKF
links.description: The AI native file format. Trust scores, source provenance, and compliance metadata that embed into 20+ formats (DOCX, PDF, images, code). EXIF for AI.

links.title: Snappy
links.description: A fast compressor/decompressor. Used with Parquet.

links.title: PigZ
links.description: A parallel implementation of gzip for modern multi-processor, multi-core machines.

links.title: SequenceFile
links.description: A flat file consisting of binary key/value pairs. It is extensively used in MapReduce as input/output formats.

links.title: PFC-JSONL
links.description: Specialized JSONL log compressor with block-level timestamp indexing and DuckDB integration. Achieves ~9% compression ratio (better than gzip) with time-range random access queries.

links.title: ParquetKit
links.description: Browser-based viewer, SQL workbench and converter for Parquet files powered by DuckDB-WASM. Fully client-side, no upload.

links.title: Apache Beam
links.description: A unified programming model that implements both batch and streaming data processing jobs that run on many execution engines.

links.title: Spark Streaming
links.description: Makes it easy to build scalable fault-tolerant streaming applications.

links.title: Apache Samza
links.description: A distributed stream processing framework.

links.title: Apache NiFi
links.description: An easy to use, powerful, and reliable system to process and distribute data.

links.title: Apache Hudi
links.description: An open source framework for managing storage for real time processing, one of the most interesting feature is the Upsert.

links.title: VoltDB
links.description: An ACID-compliant RDBMS which uses a [shared nothing architecture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shared-nothing_architecture).

links.title: PipelineDB
links.description: The Streaming SQL Database.

links.title: Spring Cloud Dataflow
links.description: Streaming and tasks execution between Spring Boot apps.

links.title: Bonobo
links.description: A data-processing toolkit for python 3.5+.

links.title: Robinhood's Faust
links.description: Forever scalable event processing & in-memory durable K/V store as a library with asyncio & static typing.

links.title: HStreamDB
links.description: The streaming database built for IoT data storage and real-time processing.

links.title: Kuiper
links.description: An edge lightweight IoT data analytics/streaming software implemented by Golang, and it can be run at all kinds of resource-constrained edge devices.

links.title: Zilla
links.description: - An API gateway built for event-driven architectures and streaming that supports standard protocols such as HTTP, SSE, gRPC, MQTT, and the native Kafka protocol.

links.title: SwimOS
links.description: A framework for building real-time streaming data processing applications that supports a wide range of ingestion sources.

links.title: Hadoop MapReduce
links.description: A software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) - in-parallel on large clusters (thousands of nodes) - of commodity hardware in a reliable, fault-tolerant manner.

links.title: Spark
links.description: A multi-language engine for executing data engineering, data science, and machine learning on single-node machines or clusters.

links.title: Spark Packages
links.description: A community index of packages for Apache Spark.

links.title: Deep Spark
links.description: Connecting Apache Spark with different data stores. Deprecated.

links.title: Spark RDD API Examples
links.description: Examples by Zhen He.

links.title: Livy
links.description: The REST Spark Server.

links.title: Delight
links.description: A free & cross platform monitoring tool (Spark UI / Spark History Server alternative).

links.title: AWS EMR
links.description: A web service that makes it easy to quickly and cost-effectively process vast amounts of data.

links.title: Data Mechanics
links.description: A cloud-based platform deployed on Kubernetes making Apache Spark more developer-friendly and cost-effective.

links.title: Tez
links.description: An application framework which allows for a complex directed-acyclic-graph of tasks for processing data.

links.title: Bistro
links.description: A light-weight engine for general-purpose data processing including both batch and stream analytics. It is based on a novel unique data model, which represents data via _functions_ and processes data via _columns operations_ as opposed to having only set operations in conventional approaches like MapReduce or SQL.

links.title: Substation
links.description: A cloud native data pipeline and transformation toolkit written in Go.

links.title: dna-claude-analysis
links.description: Personal genome analysis toolkit with Python scripts analyzing raw DNA data across 17 categories (health risks, ancestry, pharmacogenomics, nutrition, psychology, etc.) and generating a terminal-style single-page HTML visualization.

links.title: H2O
links.description: Fast scalable machine learning API for smarter applications.

links.title: Spark MLlib
links.description: Spark's scalable machine learning library consisting of common learning algorithms and utilities, including classification, regression, clustering, collaborative filtering, dimensionality reduction, as well as underlying optimization primitives.

links.title: Zingg
links.description: Open source Master Data Management platform using machine learning for entity resolution at scale. Native to Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, AWS, and GCP. Golden records are maintained through a persistent Zingg ID across all systems and sources.

links.title: GraphLab Create
links.description: A machine learning platform that enables data scientists and app developers to easily create intelligent apps at scale.

links.title: Giraph
links.description: An iterative graph processing system built for high scalability.

links.title: Spark GraphX
links.description: Apache Spark's API for graphs and graph-parallel computation.

links.title: Presto
links.description: A distributed SQL query engine designed to query large data sets distributed over one or more heterogeneous data sources.

links.title: Hive
links.description: Data warehouse software facilitates querying and managing large datasets residing in distributed storage.

links.title: Hivemall
links.description: Scalable machine learning library for Hive/Hadoop.

links.title: PyHive
links.description: Python interface to Hive and Presto.

links.title: Highcharts
links.description: A charting library written in pure JavaScript, offering an easy way of adding interactive charts to your web site or web application.

links.title: ZingChart
links.description: Fast JavaScript charts for any data set.

links.title: C3.js
links.description: D3-based reusable chart library.

links.title: D3.js
links.description: A JavaScript library for manipulating documents based on data.

links.title: D3Plus
links.description: D3's simpler, easier to use cousin. Mostly predefined templates that you can just plug data in.

links.title: SmoothieCharts
links.description: A JavaScript Charting Library for Streaming Data.

links.title: PyXley
links.description: Python helpers for building dashboards using Flask and React.

links.title: Plotly
links.description: Flask, JS, and CSS boilerplate for interactive, web-based visualization apps in Python.

links.title: Apache Superset
links.description: A modern, enterprise-ready business intelligence web application.

links.title: Redash
links.description: Make Your Company Data Driven. Connect to any data source, easily visualize and share your data.

links.title: Metabase
links.description: The easy, open source way for everyone in your company to ask questions and learn from data.

links.title: stratif.io
links.description: Open-source, self-hosted, warehouse-native product analytics. Runs funnels, retention, and paths directly on DuckDB, Postgres, Snowflake, or ClickHouse.

links.title: PyQtGraph
links.description: A pure-python graphics and GUI library built on PyQt4 / PySide and numpy. It is intended for use in mathematics / scientific / engineering applications.

links.title: Seaborn
links.description: A Python visualization library based on matplotlib. It provides a high-level interface for drawing attractive statistical graphics.

links.title: QueryGPT
links.description: Natural language database query interface with automatic chart generation, supporting Chinese and English queries.

links.title: AI for Database
links.description: Agentic AI platform to connect any database (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, etc.) and query in plain English; includes self-refreshing intelligent dashboards and action workflows triggered by data changes.

links.title: Dekart
links.description: Open-source SQL to map platform for BigQuery, Snowflake, and PostGIS.

links.title: LunaPad
links.description: Open-source analytics notebook for reusable SQL workflows, interactive reports, and AI-assisted data exploration.

links.title: Bonnard
links.description: Governed, multi-tenant MCP access to your customers' data. Turn your warehouse, dbt, or semantic layer into a secure, per-customer MCP for AI agents.

links.title: Nika
links.description: Intent-as-code workflow engine for AI data pipelines: reviewable YAML DAGs statically checked (schema, permits, cost floor) before execution, with tamper-evident run traces.

links.title: OrionBelt Semantic Layer
links.description: Open-source semantic sidecar that compiles YAML-defined dimensions, measures, and metrics into optimized SQL across 8 engines (BigQuery, ClickHouse, Databricks, Dremio, DuckDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Snowflake). Unified REST, MCP, and Postgres wire protocol; one model powers AI agents, analytics, DQ rules, and KPIs.

links.title: Bruin
links.description: End-to-end data pipeline tool that combines ingestion, transformation (SQL + Python), and data quality in a single CLI. Connects to BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, Redshift, and more. Includes VS Code extension with live previews.

links.title: DataFlow
links.description: Open-source platform for data preparation, synthetic data generation, and AI/data pipelines. Includes reusable skills for automating workflow steps across data and AI tasks.

links.title: CronQ
links.description: An application cron-like system. [Used](https://chairnerd.seatgeek.com/building-out-the-seatgeek-data-pipeline/) w/Luigi. Deprecated.

links.title: Cascading
links.description: Java based application development platform.

links.title: Azkaban
links.description: A batch workflow job scheduler created at LinkedIn to run Hadoop jobs. Azkaban resolves the ordering through job dependencies and provides an easy-to-use web user interface to maintain and track your workflows.

links.title: Oozie
links.description: A workflow scheduler system to manage Apache Hadoop jobs.

links.title: Pinball
links.description: DAG based workflow manager. Job flows are defined programmatically in Python. Support output passing between jobs.

links.title: Hamilton
links.description: A lightweight library to define data transformations as a directed-acyclic graph (DAG). If you like dbt for SQL transforms, you will like Hamilton for Python processing.

links.title: Kedro
links.description: A framework that makes it easy to build robust and scalable data pipelines by providing uniform project templates, data abstraction, configuration and pipeline assembly.

links.title: Dataform
links.description: An open-source framework and web based IDE to manage datasets and their dependencies. SQLX extends your existing SQL warehouse dialect to add features that support dependency management, testing, documentation and more.

links.title: Dotflow
links.description: A lightweight Python library for building execution pipelines with retry, parallel execution, cron scheduling, and async support.

links.title: Census
links.description: A reverse-ETL tool that let you sync data from your cloud data warehouse to SaaS applications like Salesforce, Marketo, HubSpot, Zendesk, etc. No engineering favors required—just SQL.

links.title: dbt
links.description: A command line tool that enables data analysts and engineers to transform data in their warehouses more effectively.

links.title: Kestra
links.description: Scalable, event-driven, language-agnostic orchestration and scheduling platform to manage millions of workflows declaratively in code.

links.title: RudderStack
links.description: A warehouse-first Customer Data Platform that enables you to collect data from every application, website and SaaS platform, and then activate it in your warehouse and business tools.

links.title: PACE
links.description: An open source framework that allows you to enforce agreements on how data should be accessed, used, and transformed, regardless of the data platform (Snowflake, BigQuery, DataBricks, etc.)

links.title: OneQuery
links.description: Self-hosted gateway for safe, auditable queries for agents across approved data sources.

links.title: Prefect
links.description: An orchestration and observability platform. With it, developers can rapidly build and scale resilient code, and triage disruptions effortlessly.

links.title: SuprSend
links.description: Create automated workflows and logic using API's for your notification service. Add templates, batching, preferences, inapp inbox with workflows to trigger notifications directly from your data warehouse.

links.title: Mage
links.description: Open-source data pipeline tool for transforming and integrating data.

links.title: SQLMesh
links.description: An open-source data transformation framework for managing, testing, and deploying SQL and Python-based data pipelines with version control, environment isolation, and automatic dependency resolution.

links.title: lakeFS
links.description: An open source platform that delivers resilience and manageability to object-storage based data lakes.

links.title: Project Nessie
links.description: A Transactional Catalog for Data Lakes with Git-like semantics. Works with Apache Iceberg tables.

links.title: Ilum
links.description: A modular Data Lakehouse platform that simplifies the management and monitoring of Apache Spark clusters across Kubernetes and Hadoop environments.

links.title: Gravitino
links.description: An open-source, unified metadata management for data lakes, data warehouses, and external catalogs.

links.title: FlightPath Data
links.description: FlightPath is a gateway to a data lake's bronze layer, protecting it from invalid external data file feeds as a trusted publisher.

links.title: rawquery
links.description: Managed lakehouse platform on Apache Iceberg with DuckDB query compute, S3 storage, Postgres wire protocol, and SQL transforms.

links.title: docker-logstash
links.description: A highly configurable Logstash (1.4.4) - Docker image running Elasticsearch (1.7.0) - and Kibana (3.1.2).

links.title: elasticsearch-jdbc
links.description: JDBC importer for Elasticsearch.

links.title: ZomboDB
links.description: PostgreSQL Extension that allows creating an index backed by Elasticsearch.

links.title: Gockerize
links.description: Package golang service into minimal Docker containers.

links.title: Flocker
links.description: Easily manage Docker containers & their data.

links.title: Rancher
links.description: RancherOS is a 20mb Linux distro that runs the entire OS as Docker containers.

links.title: Kontena
links.description: Application Containers for Masses.

links.title: Weave
links.description: Weaving Docker containers into applications.

links.title: Zodiac
links.description: A lightweight tool for easy deployment and rollback of dockerized applications.

links.title: cAdvisor
links.description: Analyzes resource usage and performance characteristics of running containers.

links.title: Micro S3 persistence
links.description: Docker microservice for saving/restoring volume data to S3.

links.title: Rocker-compose
links.description: Docker composition tool with idempotency features for deploying apps composed of multiple containers. Deprecated.

links.title: Nomad
links.description: A cluster manager, designed for both long-lived services and short-lived batch processing workloads.

links.title: ImageLayers
links.description: Visualize Docker images and the layers that compose them.

links.title: DexPaprika
links.description: Free real-time DEX data via SSE streaming across 34 blockchains. 30M+ pools, 27M+ tokens, ~1 second price updates. No API key, no rate limits. [Docs](https://docs.dexpaprika.com)

links.title: Helium MCP
links.description: Remote MCP server for real-time financial data, 3.2M+ news articles, ML options pricing, and news bias analysis. Free, no API key. [MCP](https://heliumtrades.com/mcp)

links.title: Twitter Realtime
links.description: The Streaming APIs give developers low latency access to Twitter's global stream of Tweet data.

links.title: Sorsa API
links.description: Real-time X (Twitter) data API providing tweets, profiles, search, communities and engagement metrics. Up to 50x cheaper than the official X API with 20 req/sec rate limit, JSON output.

links.title: Eventsim
links.description: Event data simulator. Generates a stream of pseudo-random events from a set of users, designed to simulate web traffic.

links.title: Eventum
links.description: Data generation platform for producing synthetic event streams with complex correlations.

links.title: Reddit
links.description: Real-time data is available including comments, submissions and links posted to reddit.

links.title: GitHub Archive
links.description: GitHub's public timeline since 2011, updated every hour.

links.title: Common Crawl
links.description: Open source repository of web crawl data.

links.title: Wikipedia
links.description: Wikipedia's complete copy of all wikis, in the form of Wikitext source and metadata embedded in XML. A number of raw database tables in SQL form are also available.

links.title: The Quiet-Broke Index
links.description: A 30-metro composite of US household cost burdens (housing, taxes, childcare, healthcare, transport) aggregated from Census ACS, BLS Consumer Expenditure Survey, and HUD Fair Market Rents. Open methodology, free, no email gate.

links.title: FirstData
links.description: The world's most comprehensive authoritative data source knowledge base. 160+ curated sources from governments, international organizations, and research institutions with MCP integration.

links.title: Mindweave Synthetic Business Data
links.description: 42-table synthetic SME dataset with double-entry accounting, tax compliance (AU/US/UK), and temporal realism. CSV, SQL, Parquet, SQLite. Ideal for ETL pipeline testing.

links.title: LatAm Synth
links.description: Synthetic financial savings behavior generator for Latin America: users, savings goals, and transactions calibrated on 506K real records (2015–2024). Reproducible by seed, 100% synthetic.

links.title: HAProxy Exporter
links.description: Simple server that scrapes HAProxy stats and exports them via HTTP for Prometheus consumption.

links.title: Signals CLI
links.description: Intent signal monitoring CLI. Track LinkedIn engagers, keyword posters, job changers, funding events. JSON output for data pipelines.

links.title: Data Profiler
links.description: The DataProfiler is a Python library designed to make data analysis, monitoring, and sensitive data detection easy.

links.title: YData Profiling
links.description: A general-purpose open-source data profiler for high-level analysis of a dataset.

links.title: Aegis DQ
links.description: Open-source agentic data quality framework with LLM-powered diagnosis, root-cause analysis, SQL auto-fix proposals, and 31 rule types — DuckDB, Postgres, BigQuery, Databricks, Athena, Snowflake.

links.title: Grai
links.description: A data catalog tool that integrates into your CI system exposing downstream impact testing of data changes. These tests prevent data changes which might break data pipelines or BI dashboards from making it to production.

links.title: DQOps
links.description: An open-source data quality platform for the whole data platform lifecycle from profiling new data sources to applying full automation of data quality monitoring.

links.title: DataKitchen
links.description: Open Source Data Observability for end-to-end Data Journey Observability, data profiling, anomaly detection, and auto-created data quality validation tests.

links.title: GreatExpectation
links.description: Open Source data validation framework to manage data quality. Users can define and document “expectations” rules about how data should look and behave.

links.title: Provero
links.description: A vendor-neutral, declarative data quality engine. Define checks in YAML, run anywhere. Includes 16 built-in check types, SQL batch optimizer, anomaly detection, and data contracts.

links.title: Scherlok
links.description: Zero-config data quality CLI. Profiles every table on first run, then auto-detects anomalies (volume drops, schema drift, freshness misses, distribution shifts) on subsequent runs. No YAML, no rules to write. Works with Postgres, BigQuery, Snowflake, and dbt.

links.title: RunSQL
links.description: Free online SQL playground for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server. Create database structures, run queries, and share results instantly.

links.title: Spark Playground
links.description: Write, run, and test PySpark code on Spark Playground's online compiler. Access real-world sample datasets & solve interview questions to enhance your PySpark skills for data engineering roles.

links.title: daffy
links.description: Decorator-first DataFrame contracts/validation (columns/dtypes/constraints) at function boundaries. Supports Pandas/Polars/PyArrow/Modin.

links.title: Snowflake Emulator
links.description: A Snowflake-compatible emulator for local development and testing.

links.title: DataScreenIQ
links.description: Real-time data quality firewall for pipelines and APIs. Screens rows in milliseconds for schema drift, null spikes, type mismatches, and data anomalies with PASS / WARN / BLOCK decisions.

links.title: DataDriven
links.description: Interview practice with SQL query execution, Python, and data modeling exercises.

links.title: Fixzi
links.description: JSON/XML validation and API contract monitoring tool for debugging and testing structured data.

links.title: /r/dataengineering
links.description: News, tips, and background on Data Engineering.

links.title: /r/etl
links.description: Subreddit focused on ETL.

links.title: AI Dev Jobs
links.description: Job board focused on AI, ML, and data engineering roles with 7,400+ listings, salary data, and a free REST API.

links.title: Data Council
links.description: The first technical conference that bridges the gap between data scientists, data engineers and data analysts.

links.title: Chain of Thought
links.description: Interviews with AI and data infrastructure leaders on building production systems.

links.title: Data Engineering Podcast
links.description: The show about modern data infrastructure.

links.title: Latent Space
links.description: Technical deep dives on AI engineering, from model training to deployment.

links.title: Practical AI
links.description: Making AI practical, productive, and accessible to everyone.

links.title: Software Engineering Daily
links.description: Daily interviews about technical software topics, including data infrastructure.

links.title: The Analytics Engineering Podcast
links.description: How analytics engineers build and maintain data pipelines at scale.

links.title: The Data Stack Show
links.description: A show where they talk to data engineers, analysts, and data scientists about their experience around building and maintaining data infrastructure, delivering data and data products, and driving better outcomes across their businesses with data.

links.title: Snowflake Data Engineering
links.description: A practical introduction to data engineering on the Snowflake cloud data platform.

links.title: Best Data Science Books
links.description: This blog offers a curated list of top data science books, categorized by topics and learning stages, to aid readers in building foundational knowledge and staying updated with industry trends.

links.title: Architecting an Apache Iceberg Lakehouse
links.description: A guide to designing an Apache Iceberg lakehouse from scratch.

links.title: Learn AI Data Engineering in a Month of Lunches
links.description: A fast, friendly guide to integrating large language models into your data workflows.
